title: Tamil Plain Dosa

ingredients.name: fermented idli-dosa batter
visual_checks: well-fermented for best crispiness; thin with a few tbsp water if needed
ingredients.notes: well-fermented for best crispiness; thin with a few tbsp water if needed

subs.name: For instant dosa: blend 1 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup urad-dal flour, 1 tbsp curd and salt with enough water into a thin batter; use immediately without fermenting for a crispier, less tangy result.
subs.notes: flavour is different but passable for a quick weekday dosa

ingredients.name: oil or ghee
visual_checks: for the tawa; gingelly oil gives authentic flavour
ingredients.notes: for the tawa; gingelly oil gives authentic flavour

subs.name: gingelly-oil
subs.notes: traditional choice; gives a nutty sesame note and a crispier, darker crust

steps.text: Season the tawa first if using a new pan: heat it dry on high flame until smoking, wipe with a cut onion or damp cloth, then rub a few drops of oil across the surface. For a well-seasoned iron tawa, simply heat on medium-high.

Mix salt into the batter and thin with a few tablespoons of water until it flows like whole milk — the consistency is what lets you spread a thin dosa. The batter should not be thick enough to hold its shape on a ladle.

When the tawa is hot and a drop of water on the surface sizzles instantly, lower the flame to medium. Pour one ladle (about 3–4 tbsp) of batter onto the centre and immediately spread it outward in quick, circular strokes with the back of the ladle.
Drizzle 0.5 tsp oil around the edges of the dosa and a little on top. Cook on medium flame until the edges start to lift and turn golden, and the surface looks dry and matte — about 2–3 minutes.

Slide a flat spatula under one edge and fold or roll the dosa. It should release cleanly; if it sticks, cook for another 30 seconds. Serve immediately on a hot plate — dosa turns soggy if it sits. Pair with coconut chutney and sambar.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Fermentation conditions affect safety. Discard batches with unexpected mould, putrid odour or other spoilage signs.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: idli-dosa-batter → For instant dosa: blend 1 cup rice flour, 0.25 cup urad-dal flour, 1 tbsp curd and salt with enough water into a thin batter; use immediately without fermenting for a crispier, less tangy result. (flavour is different but passable for a quick weekday dosa); oil → gingelly-oil (traditional choice; gives a nutty sesame note and a crispier, darker crust)
